description: Vertical-split layout — portrait-pinned image (3:4) filling the left 58% column, narrative text in the right 42% column on pure cream. Type B (no region detection, no auto-fit, static CSS). Composition prompt asks Gemini for vertical-emphasis scenes (looking up, towering elements, vertical scale) — the catalogue's portrait-image-orientation slot, distinct from prompt-2's landscape side-by-side. Aspect-pinned to 3:4 so column aspect matches image aspect exactly (zero crop). Validated 2026-05-24 over N=3 (Milky Way / towering tree / stress: indoor-reading-looking-down) — pin held identically across all 3 (0.747 vs 0.750 target), Gemini composed vertically even on the stress scene (tall room interpretation for a low/seated/looking-down action). Catalogue's first portrait-image template + first template on the side-by-side-with-portrait arrangement axis.

selection_metadata.summary: Vertical-split layout. Portrait-pinned image on the left, narrative text in a pure-cream column on the right. Vertical-emphasis compositions (looking up, towering sky, vertical scale).

imageGeneration.customCompositionRules: single focal subject, vertical composition, face clearly visible, the vertical axis of the composition aligned with the subject's gaze or gesture.
imageGeneration.compositionPromptTemplate:
The image is a 3:4 PORTRAIT frame — taller than wide. This is a vertical composition. The painted scene fills the portrait frame edge-to-edge; compose to USE the full vertical scale of the frame.

VERTICAL COMPOSITION INTENT: This composition's expressive purpose is VERTICAL SCALE and reaching-upward energy. The composition should emphasize VERTICAL movement, scale, or gaze: the protagonist LOOKING UP, towering elements rising ABOVE the subject (vast sky, tall trees, rising stars, soaring clouds), or the subject framed against a TALL vertical backdrop. The UPPER portion of the frame is where the vertical scale unfolds — the towering sky, the rising elements, the vast above. The LOWER portion is where the protagonist is grounded.

SUBJECT PLACEMENT: protagonist centered horizontally in the portrait frame, positioned in the LOWER HALF of the frame (roughly bottom 40% — feet on the ground), looking UP or reaching UP toward the towering content above. Face clearly visible.

Full painted scene edge-to-edge — atmospheric {{MEDIUM:medium}} {{MEDIUM:washWord}} extending to all four edges of the portrait frame. No cream margins, no contained vignette. Keep the protagonist and key action toward the LEFT and CENTER of the portrait; the RIGHT edge of the frame should be the calmest, most atmospheric part (soft sky, distant {{MEDIUM:washWord}}, negative space), because that edge feathers into the cream paper where the story text sits — let the painting melt toward the text rather than ending on a hard edge.
